message:
feat(install): Major v4.6.0 release with Fish shell support and enhanced robustness

Upgrade installation script from v4.5 to v4.6.0 with comprehensive improvements
to shell compatibility, error handling, installation modes, and user experience.

Version and globals:
- Bumped VERSION to 4.6.0 reflecting significant feature additions
- Added ORIGINAL_ARGS global array for update re-exec workflow
- Added WORKDIR for isolated temporary file handling
- New flags: QUIET, SYSTEM_WIDE, NO_PATH_MODIFY
- Better umask 022 and shopt -s lastpipe for consistency

Shell compatibility enhancements:
- Fish shell support: detect_shell() now recognizes fish via $SHELL
- get_rc_file() returns ~/.config/fish/config.fish for fish users
- Fish-specific PATH modification: "set -gx PATH $PATH <dir>"
- Fish-specific command alias: function-based instead of alias keyword
- Maintains backward compatibility with bash, zsh, and POSIX shells

Color and TTY handling:
- init_colors() function: Centralized color initialization logic
- NO_COLOR, TTY, and TERM detection for safer color decisions
- COLOR_ENABLED flag prevents color codes in pipes or dumb terminals
- Conditional color assignment: only define when COLOR_ENABLED=1

Installation modes:
- --system-wide: Install to /usr/local/bin instead of ~/.local/bin
- --no-path-modify: Skip PATH modification entirely (CI/container use)
- --quiet: Suppress non-essential output for automated environments
- maybe_sudo() helper: Automatically use sudo only when needed based on
  directory writability and sudo availability

Download and retry logic:
- with_backoff(): Exponential backoff retry wrapper (default 3 attempts)
- download_to_file(): Unified curl/wget download with retry support
- All GitHub fetches now use with_backoff for transient network failures
- Better error logging: /tmp/download-error.log captures failure details

Sudo and permissions:
- maybe_sudo() returns "sudo" only if directory needs elevated privileges
- Better can_use_sudo(): Checks both availability and passwordless capability
- install -m 0755 usage when sudo required for atomic copy+chmod
- Separate sudo invocations for mkdir, install, chmod when needed

Update mechanism improvements:
- ORIGINAL_ARGS preservation: Re-exec passes through all original flags
- Conditional exec: Handle both "${ORIGINAL_ARGS[@]}" and no-args cases
- LOCK_OWNED=0 before rmdir: Prevents race condition in signal handlers
- Better error handling if exec fails after releasing lock

Binary download enhancements:
- jq macOS ARM support: Added jq-macos-arm64 asset detection
- Better asset selection: Platform and architecture-specific matching
- PATH export after binary install: Ensures tools available in current session
- Improved archive extraction: Robust find commands for nested binaries

Version comparison refinements:
- Strip 'v' prefix: Handle v4.5.0 and 4.5.0 formats consistently
- sort -V validation: Test before use to handle BSD/macOS differences
- Fallback numeric comparison: Strip non-digits for pure version component
  comparison when sort -V unavailable
- Prevent 10#X errors: Explicit base-10 arithmetic to avoid octal interpretation

Verification and smoke testing:
- had_ubs flag: Track if ubs available before running smoke test
- Conditional smoke test: Only run if ubs successfully installed
- grep -E usage: Portable extended regex instead of -E in some grep variants
- Better test file cleanup: Always remove temporary test files

Cleanup and temporary file handling:
- WORKDIR: mktemp -d for isolated workspace, cleaned on EXIT trap
- TEMP_FILES array tracking: Register all temp paths for guaranteed cleanup
- Better rm -rf guards: Check variable defined before removal
- Trap consolidation: INT/TERM exit with proper codes (130/143)

Shell RC file modification:
- sed -i.bak portability: Works on both Linux and macOS
- Multiple pattern removal: Handle comment lines, export lines, and orphaned
  blank lines separately
- fish function cleanup: Remove "function ubs" blocks properly
- Better blank line removal: sed '/^$/N;/^\n$/d' for consecutive blank lines

Configuration and diagnostics:
- normalize_bool(): Convert yes/no/true/false/1/0 consistently
- read_config_file(): Simple key=value parser with comment support
- diagnostic_check(): Enhanced with lsb_release output when available
- Better system info: Distro, OS, platform, architecture, shell details

Installation validation:
- File size checks: Warn if < 2KB instead of hard failing (allows smaller
  meta-runners)
- Content marker flexibility: Check for both "UBS Meta-Runner" and
  "Ultimate Bug Scanner" to handle naming variations
- UTF-8 BOM stripping: sed with octal escapes for cross-platform compatibility
- Shebang validation: Strip BOM, then verify #!/.*bash pattern

PATH handling improvements:
- is_in_path(): Robust checking with realpath normalization
- Handles symlinks: realpath -s to compare canonical paths
- Trailing slash normalization: Remove trailing / for consistent comparison
- IFS=':' safety: Local IFS override for PATH iteration

Alias and command setup:
- Fish function syntax: multi-line function definition with $argv
- Bash/zsh alias: Traditional alias ubs='<path>' format
- Verification: Check if ubs command available before adding alias
- Update detection: Compare existing alias target and update if changed

Error messages and logging:
- Consistent error format: "${RED}${BOLD}...${RESET}" patterns
- Better command logging: Show actual command in error context
- Download failure logging: /tmp/download-error.log for debugging
- Timeout logging: Capture timeout kill signals for troubleshooting

Portability fixes:
- BSD/macOS sed compatibility: Always use -i.bak for in-place edits
- Process group killing: kill -TERM -$pid for safer timeout implementation
- locale checks: grep -qiE for both C.UTF-8 and en_US.UTF-8
- Command availability: command -v instead of which for portability

Code quality and safety:
- Removed "eval" for date arithmetic: Direct bash arithmetic instead
- Better quoting: "${var}" throughout for spaces in paths
- Conditional chaining: && and || for safer multi-command sequences
- Guard clauses: [ -n "${var:-}" ] before usage to prevent unbound errors

This release significantly improves the installation experience across
diverse environments (Linux, macOS, WSL, BSD, Fish users, CI systems) while
maintaining backward compatibility and adding important safety features.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+with_backoff() {
+  # with_backoff <max_attempts> <cmd...>
+  local max="${1:-3}"; shift || true
+  local n=1 delay=1
+  while true; do

+    if (( n >= max )); then return 1; fi
+    sleep "$delay"
+    n=$((n+1)); delay=$((delay*2))
